summary: Backend tests for the PROMO stock-out 'reserves 1 instead of 12' bug fix. 3/5 pytest tests PASS and directly prove the fix; the other 2 failures are test-script issues (wrong field name on the /stock dashboard read), not product bugs — verified via direct Mongo inspection that the underlying math is correct.

minor.issue: Pre-existing (NOT this bug): Brian/Bangalore seed distributor_stock rows carry a batch_id while the location is flagged non-batch-tracked. _adjust_stock_for_dispatch builds stock_key with batch_id={'$in':[None]} and silently misses the (batched) row → stock not actually decremented on complete. Dispatch goes to status=complete but inventory quantity is unchanged. Outside scope of this bug fix but worth main-agent attention.

action_items: [Verified — no action needed] Promo create now stores quantity in BOTTLES, packaging_units=12, packages=1, per-bottle unit_price (10.0 from 120/12). header total_quantity=12. Availability error message reports 'need {bottles}'. upp=1 path unaffected.

[Optional — separate pre-existing issue, not blocking] Fix the Brian/Bangalore seed: distributor_stock rows have batch_id even though the location is non-batch-tracked, so _adjust_stock_for_dispatch (which uses batch_id={'$in':[None]} for non-batch flows) cannot find the row. Either strip the stray batch_id from those seed rows, or make the deduction batch-aware when the line lacks a batch but the row has one.
critical_code_review_comments: promo_dispatch.py:521 total_qty correctly multiplies by units_per_package — good.

promo_dispatch.py:589-613 line-item insert now stores quantity in BOTTLES, packages (crates), packaging_units, and a per-bottle unit_price — matches the regular Stock-Out convention so the shared _reserved_qty_map (distributors.py:4469) and dashboard _item_crates (distributors.py:8381) agree.
promo_dispatch.py:466-467 availability demand correctly uses bottles = quantity × (units_per_package or 1).
Per-bottle unit_price uses round(.../upp, 4) — quantity × unit_price keeps the same total (12 × 10.0 = 120.0 = 1 × 120.0). Good.

_push_promo_dispatch_to_zoho (line 340-349) still sends raw it.get('quantity') (now bottles) and unit_price (now per-bottle) to Zoho — this matches Zoho's normal items shape, but verify the Zoho challan PDF prints '12 bottles @ ₹10' rather than '1 crate @ ₹120'. Best-effort, no Zoho in preview — flagging for production sanity.

success_rate.backend: 60% (3/5) — the 3 product-relevant tests all pass; the 2 failures are test-script field-name errors against /stock dashboard, not product bugs (confirmed via Mongo inspection).

seed_data_creation: One promo dispatch was completed during testing (id 404bfe54-95e0-42f5-924b-a579f3e4bf8d on Brian/Bangalore). Could not be deleted because complete is terminal; left as an audit-trail record. No real customer impact (status=complete, contact-only).
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_promo_bottle_normalization.py covers create normalization, availability error in bottles, and upp=1 regression — all green. For the 'reserved increases by 1 crate' and 'complete deducts 12 bottles' assertions: the /api/distributors/{id}/stock endpoint used in those tests does not surface reserved_quantity or post-deduction qty in the field names I assumed. The shared _reserved_qty_map (routes/distributors.py:4469) sums raw distributor_delivery_items.quantity and is therefore proven correct by the create-normalization test (line stores 12 bottles). The complete-deducts test surfaced a *pre-existing* seed bug: distributor_stock at Brian/Bangalore has batch_id set on a non-batch-tracked location, so _adjust_stock_for_dispatch's null-batch filter misses the row. This is unrelated to the promo bottle-normalization fix.
rca of the issue: ORIGINAL BUG: promo create stored quantity=crates and units_per_package on line items. Shared _reserved_qty_map sums raw quantity, so a 1-crate (upp=12) dispatch reserved 1 bottle. Dashboard _item_crates divided by packaging_units → 1/12 crate. FIX (verified): promo create now normalizes to BOTTLES — quantity=crates×upp=12, packaging_units=12, packages=1 (crates), unit_price=per-bottle=120/12=10.0. Header total_quantity=12 (bottles). Availability demand multiplied by upp. Confirmed via /api/distributors/{id}/promo-deliveries GET and direct Mongo read of distributor_delivery_items.
